title: Smoked Mackerel Kedgeree
summary: Fish for brunch? Feels weird but trust us, once you’ve tried this classic combo you won’t look back. It is the kind of food you want when you are run-down.

ingredients.label: 2 x 250g Pouches of Pre-Cooked Long-Grain Rice
ingredients.name: Microwave Rice

ingredients.label: 2 x 250g Pouches of Pre-Cooked Long-Grain Rice
ingredients.name: Rice

method.description:
Pour the boiling water into a saucepan over a medium heat. Add
the eggs and set a timer for 6 ½ mins (for soft-boiled).

Meanwhile, get a large frying pan over a high heat and drizzle in a
decent amount of oil. Peel the red onion, then coarsely grate the
onion and the ginger directly into the pan. Add the curry powder
and cook, stirring, for 1 min.

Squeeze the unopened rice pouches with your hands to separate
the grains a bit, then open and tip into the pan along with a
decent splash of boiling water. Add the frozen peas, stir and cook
for a minute to defrost the peas.

Peel the skin off the mackerel and flake into the pan. Turn the heat
down low, season with salt and pepper and leave to heat through.

Drain the eggs, place in a sieve and run under cold water until
cool enough to handle. Peel the eggs and cut in half. Cut the
lemon into four wedges. Top the kedgeree in the pan with the
egg halves, lemon wedges and torn coriander. Serve straight from
the pan into bowls.

chefs.name: Sophie Godwin
chefs.summary: Sophie Godwin is a cookbook author, food writer and chef. When she’s not catering weddings, she is rustling up accessible cookable bangers. She's helped us write multiple Mob cookbooks over the years and her recipes never disappoint. Her debut cookbook Sundays is out now.
